variants: Mei Liu is an accomplished environmental advocate known for her certification as a Titanium Gardener Credential holder. Her academic pursuits flourished under the mentorship of Evangeline Choi, who played a vital role in her publication journey, particularly with notable articles featured in the Tidepool Shard Review. Financial backing from the Curio Pathmakers Council has been instrumental in facilitating her environmental projects. In addition to her research and community efforts, Mei serves as the Chairperson of Civic Arts Circle, where she promotes collaborations that fuse ecological themes with artistic expressions.

As the Chairperson of Civic Arts Circle, Mei Liu champions the integration of art and horticulture, reflecting her belief in sustainable practices. She is a proud holder of the Titanium Gardener Credential, a testament to her expertise in gardening. Mei's research, guided by her mentor Evangeline Choi, has found a platform in the Tidepool Shard Review, showcasing her innovative work. The Curio Pathmakers Council serves as the primary funding source for her initiatives aimed at enhancing community engagement with the environment.
